goal: On the single-card share posters (Daily + Ask-a-Question 1-card), replace the big italic-serif card-name headline with a short MEANING RECAP, since the card art already prints the name.
done: Added resolveCardImplyRecap(card,lang,3) in share/poster.js (top-3 localized tarot_imply keywords, EN/TH, falls back to EN card name). Wired into Daily drawCardName and Quick-Pull renderQuickPullPoster. Then fixed a TH centring regression: the recap is now Thai but the headline still used textAlign='center' (iOS-Safari Thai mis-centre -> drifts off right edge); switched both renderers to explicit centring (textAlign='left' + width/2 - lineWidth/2), matching the tagline. Bumped share/index.html cache-bust 20260623r->20260624a to deliver fresh poster.js past the edge cache. Mirrored to www/ and ios/.
root_cause: Thai text drawn with canvas textAlign='center' mis-centres on iOS Safari and left-aligns past the right edge; the headline only became Thai once it switched from the always-English card name to the localized imply recap.
fix: Explicit centring (left-align + per-line width/2 - lineWidth/2) in Daily drawCardName and Quick-Pull cardName block; recap sourced from tarot_imply via getLocalizedField.
verified: Headless render of Daily TH (Two of Cups, Four of Swords) shows the 3-keyword recap centred; Daily EN + Ask-Q TH render correctly. Live needed the ?v bump because poster.js sits behind a multi-hour unhashed edge cache.
